front: Work: argues the body is a subject, not just an object — the means by which we experience the world; rejects Cartesian mind-body dualism in favor of Husserl's method
Philosopher: examined phantom limb feelings in amputees to develop the concept of 'body image' in his magnum opus
Work: inspired Iris Marion Young's 'Throwing Like a Girl,' arguing women have 'inhibited intentionality' in physical tasks
Work: unfinished, posthumous work developing the 'ontology of flesh,' distinguishing 'flesh of the body' from 'flesh of the world'; last chapter is 'The Intertwining — The Chiasm'
Work: an essay examining Cezanne's shyness and solitary devotion to painting, analyzing his 'lived perspective'
work: Cezanne's Doubt and Eye and Mind

front: Work: argues that a painter 'offers himself or herself' to the world; criticizes 'operational thinking'
work: Cezanne's Doubt and Eye and Mind

front: Philosopher: in Adventures of the Dialectic, criticized Sartre's 'Ultrabolshevism' and condemned Kojeve's 'end of history'
Philosopher: co-edited Les Temps Modernes with Sartre but quit after the Korean War; Sartre wrote a eulogy titled '[this philosopher] Vivant'
